topics.title: The United Nations Security Council
topics.description: The most powerful body in the UN, with 5 permanent members holding veto power. It decides on peace, sanctions, and military action worldwide.

topics.title: How Elections Work in Different Countries
topics.description: From proportional representation to first-past-the-post — discover how democracies around the world choose their leaders differently.

topics.title: The G20 Summit
topics.description: The G20 brings together the world's 20 largest economies to discuss global financial stability, climate change, and trade policies.

topics.title: NATO — North Atlantic Treaty Organization
topics.description: A military alliance of 31 countries. An attack on one is an attack on all. Learn how this shapes modern geopolitics.

topics.title: What Is a Veto Power?
topics.description: Some countries have the power to block any decision unilaterally. Learn which nations hold veto powers and why this is controversial.

topics.title: The History of the European Union
topics.description: From post-WWII coal agreements to a 27-nation political union with a common currency — the EU's story is fascinating.

topics.title: What Is Geopolitics?
topics.description: How geography shapes politics, trade, and military power. Why controlling certain seas, mountains, or resources changes world history.

topics.title: How the IMF Works
topics.description: The International Monetary Fund lends money to struggling countries. But there are strings attached. Learn how it shapes global economies.

topics.title: What Is Inflation?
topics.description: Why does money lose value over time? Understand inflation, its causes, and why central banks target 2% — not 0%.

topics.title: The Silk Road — Ancient Trade Routes
topics.description: For centuries, merchants traveled thousands of miles connecting China to Rome. This road spread goods, religions, and diseases across continents.

topics.title: The Cold War Explained
topics.description: Decades of tension between the US and USSR without direct fighting. Learn about the arms race, proxy wars, and the Space Race.

topics.title: The French Revolution
topics.description: How an economic crisis and inequality led to the overthrow of a monarchy and reshaped the concept of human rights forever.

topics.title: The British Empire at Its Peak
topics.description: At its largest, Britain controlled 25% of Earth's land. Explore how this empire formed, ruled, and eventually collapsed.

topics.title: The Partition of India
topics.description: In 1947, British India split into two nations — India and Pakistan. One of the largest mass migrations in human history followed.

topics.title: The Marshall Plan
topics.description: After WWII, the US sent billions to rebuild Europe. But there was a strategic reason too — learn how it shaped the modern world.

topics.title: Mongolia — The World's Least Dense Country
topics.description: Mongolia is larger than France and Germany combined but has a tiny population. Nomadic culture, the Gobi Desert, and Genghis Khan's legacy.

topics.title: Bhutan — The Happiness Kingdom
topics.description: Bhutan measures success by Gross National Happiness instead of GDP. Learn how this Himalayan kingdom thinks about development differently.

topics.title: Iceland — Fire, Ice, and Vikings
topics.description: A volcanic island with glaciers, the world's oldest parliament, and near 100% renewable energy. Iceland is unlike anywhere else.

topics.title: Ethiopia — Africa's Oldest Nation
topics.description: One of the few African countries never colonized, Ethiopia has a unique calendar, alphabet, and ancient Christian history.

topics.title: Singapore — From Fishing Village to Megacity
topics.description: In just 50 years, Singapore transformed into one of the richest nations on Earth. Learn the story of this remarkable city-state.

topics.title: Norway's Sovereign Wealth Fund
topics.description: Norway found oil and, instead of spending it, saved it. The result? A $1.4 trillion fund that owns 1% of all global stocks.

topics.title: The Amazon Rainforest
topics.description: Home to 10% of all species on Earth, producing 20% of the world's oxygen. Discover why this ecosystem is called 'the lungs of the Earth'.

topics.title: How Black Holes Work
topics.description: A region in space where gravity is so strong that even light can't escape. How do they form? What happens if you fall into one?

topics.title: The Human Immune System
topics.description: Your body fights millions of threats every day without you knowing. Learn how white blood cells, antibodies, and memory cells protect you.

topics.title: Plate Tectonics — The Moving Earth
topics.description: Earth's crust is broken into giant pieces that move. This explains earthquakes, volcanoes, and why South America fits into Africa.

topics.title: CRISPR — Editing the Code of Life
topics.description: Scientists can now edit DNA like text. CRISPR could cure genetic diseases — but also raises serious ethical questions.

topics.title: How Vaccines Work
topics.description: Vaccines teach your immune system to fight diseases before you encounter them. One of medicine's greatest achievements, explained simply.

topics.title: The Ocean's Depths — The Mariana Trench
topics.description: Deeper than Mount Everest is tall, the Mariana Trench is Earth's deepest point. Strange creatures live there in total darkness.

topics.title: Why the Sky Is Blue
topics.description: It's not water vapor or reflection. Light scatters differently through the atmosphere. A simple physics lesson with a beautiful answer.

topics.title: Universal Basic Income
topics.description: What if every citizen received a regular cash payment, no conditions? Countries are experimenting with it. Here are the results.

topics.title: The Caste System in India
topics.description: A social hierarchy thousands of years old that still affects millions today. Understanding its history, legal status, and cultural impact.

topics.title: How the Internet Actually Works
topics.description: What really happens when you type a URL? From DNS servers to fiber optic cables under the ocean — the internet is physical.

topics.title: The History of the Printing Press
topics.description: Gutenberg's press changed everything. It made books affordable, spread literacy, and directly led to the Reformation and Scientific Revolution.

topics.title: What Is Artificial Intelligence?
topics.description: From rule-based systems to neural networks — how machines learn, what they can and can't do, and what the future might hold.

topics.title: Nuclear Power — How It Works
topics.description: Splitting atoms releases enormous energy. Nuclear plants produce clean power but leave radioactive waste for thousands of years. Worth it?

topics.title: The Panama Canal
topics.description: A waterway that cut 8,000 miles off shipping routes. Building it cost tens of thousands of lives. How it changed global trade forever.

topics.title: The Pyramids of Giza
topics.description: Built 4,500 years ago with no modern tools. How did ancient Egyptians move 2-million stone blocks? Theories continue to surprise us.

topics.title: How GPS Works
topics.description: Your phone knows exactly where you are using signals from satellites 20,000 km above Earth. Time, math, and physics make it possible.

topics.title: The Human Brain
topics.description: 86 billion neurons, 100 trillion connections. The most complex object in the known universe — and it fits in your skull.

topics.title: The Story of Writing
topics.description: Writing was invented independently several times. From Sumerian cuneiform to alphabets — how humans learned to record thought.

topics.title: What Is Democracy?
topics.description: Ancient Athens invented it, Rome modified it, and modern nations have 100 different versions. What does democracy really mean?

topics.title: The Global Water Crisis
topics.description: Over 2 billion people lack access to safe drinking water. Where is water running out, why, and what are solutions?

topics.title: The Space Race
topics.description: Two superpowers competed to conquer space. Sputnik, Gagarin, Apollo 11 — these milestones changed technology and human ambition forever.

topics.title: How Money Was Invented
topics.description: Humans traded before currency existed. From barter to coins to paper money to digital — the evolution of how we exchange value.

topics.title: The Philosophy of Stoicism
topics.description: Focus only on what you can control. Used by Roman emperors and modern executives alike. Ancient wisdom for modern life.

topics.title: The United Nations — How It Works
topics.description: Founded after WWII to prevent another global war, the UN has 193 member states. But can it actually enforce anything?

topics.title: Climate Change — The Science
topics.description: Why CO2 traps heat, how fast temperatures are rising, what feedback loops are, and what the models predict for 2100.

topics.title: The Renaissance
topics.description: A rebirth of art, science, and thinking in Europe after the Middle Ages. Da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Copernicus all lived in this era.

topics.title: What Is a Constitution?
topics.description: A country's highest law. Some are long (India: 145,000 words), some short (USA: 7,591 words). What makes a good constitution?

topics.title: The Columbian Exchange
topics.description: When Columbus reached America, two worlds of plants, animals, and diseases collided. Potatoes, tomatoes, and smallpox changed everything.

topics.title: How Airplanes Fly
topics.description: It's not just about lift. Learn the aerodynamics of flight — how wings, engines, and the laws of physics keep 500 tons airborne.

topics.title: The Philosophy of Existentialism
topics.description: Life has no inherent meaning — you create your own. Sartre, Camus, and Kierkegaard asked: what does it mean to exist?

topics.title: How the Stock Market Works
topics.description: Companies sell pieces of themselves to raise money. Investors bet on their future. Learn what moves prices and why markets crash.

topics.title: The History of Antibiotics
topics.description: Fleming's accidental discovery of penicillin in 1928 saved hundreds of millions of lives. Now bacteria are fighting back.

topics.title: The Rwandan Genocide
topics.description: In 100 days in 1994, 800,000 people were killed. Understanding how propaganda and colonial history turned neighbors against each other.

topics.title: What Is Nuclear Deterrence?
topics.description: If both sides have nukes, neither will use them — or so the theory goes. How MAD (Mutually Assured Destruction) shapes world peace.

topics.title: New Zealand — First Country to Give Women the Vote
topics.description: In 1893, New Zealand became the first self-governing country where women could vote. A small nation with outsized global influence.

topics.title: The Gut Microbiome
topics.description: Your gut contains 38 trillion bacteria. They affect your mood, immunity, and even your decisions. Meet your inner ecosystem.

topics.title: Mahatma Gandhi and Civil Disobedience
topics.description: How one man used nonviolence to defeat the British Empire. Gandhi's methods inspired Martin Luther King Jr. and movements worldwide.

topics.title: The Theory of Evolution
topics.description: How life on Earth diversified over billions of years through natural selection. Darwin's idea that changed everything we know about biology.

topics.title: What Is a Central Bank?
topics.description: The Reserve Bank of India, the US Federal Reserve — these institutions control money supply and interest rates for entire economies.

topics.title: How Computers Work
topics.description: From transistors to binary code to operating systems — how does typing a key ultimately make something appear on your screen?

topics.title: Bhagavad Gita — Philosophy of Action
topics.description: One of humanity's greatest philosophical texts. On the battlefield, Krishna explains duty, action without attachment, and the eternal self.

topics.title: The History of India's Independence
topics.description: A 90-year struggle against British rule, involving millions of people, two world wars, and a midnight moment that changed the world.

topics.title: Caste and the Indian Constitution
topics.description: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar designed India's constitution to dismantle caste discrimination. How well has it worked 75 years later?

topics.title: The Green Revolution
topics.description: In the 1960s, new wheat and rice varieties saved over a billion people from starvation. Norman Borlaug — the man who fed the world.

topics.title: How Photosynthesis Works
topics.description: Plants turn sunlight, water, and CO2 into sugar and oxygen. This one chemical reaction is the foundation of almost all life on Earth.

topics.title: The History of Cryptocurrency
topics.description: Bitcoin was created in 2009 by an anonymous person. How does blockchain work, and what's the argument for decentralized money?

topics.title: The Roman Empire's Fall
topics.description: The greatest empire in history collapsed over centuries. Was it barbarians, economic collapse, or something else? Historians still debate.

topics.title: What Is Quantum Mechanics?
topics.description: At the smallest scales, particles are in multiple states at once until observed. The theory that powers lasers, MRI machines, and computers.

topics.title: The United States Electoral College
topics.description: Americans don't directly elect their president. Learn how the Electoral College works, why it was created, and why it's controversial.

topics.title: What Is Federalism?
topics.description: Power shared between a central government and states. India, USA, Germany all use it differently. Why it works — and when it fails.

topics.title: The History of the Internet
topics.description: From a 1969 US military network to something 5 billion people use daily. The fascinating story of how the web was born.

topics.title: Meditation and the Science Behind It
topics.description: Neuroscience shows meditation physically changes the brain. What happens during meditation and what benefits does research actually confirm?

topics.title: The Story of Coffee
topics.description: Discovered by Ethiopian goats, popularized by Arabian traders, spread by European colonists. How coffee conquered the world.

topics.title: Antarctica — A Continent Governed by No Country
topics.description: 70 countries have signed a treaty making Antarctica a scientific reserve. No military, no mining, no ownership. How does this work?

topics.title: The Industrial Revolution
topics.description: Steam, steel, and factories transformed human life in just 100 years. It created wealth — and misery — on an unprecedented scale.

topics.title: Philosophy of Utilitarianism
topics.description: The right action is whatever creates the most happiness for the most people. Simple? Bentham and Mill explore the complications.

topics.title: How Language Shapes Thought
topics.description: Does the language you speak affect how you think? Some languages have no word for 'left' or 'right' — and speakers navigate differently.

topics.title: The Role of the Supreme Court
topics.description: Why do unelected judges have so much power? How constitutional courts balance democracy with protection of individual rights.

topics.title: The Story of Salt
topics.description: Salt was once so valuable it was used as currency. Wars were fought over it. Gandhi's Salt March brought down an empire.

topics.title: How Trade Routes Shaped Civilizations
topics.description: Whoever controlled trade routes controlled the world. How the spice trade funded Columbus and built the modern global economy.

topics.title: Human Rights — The Universal Declaration
topics.description: In 1948, after the Holocaust, the world agreed on 30 basic human rights. Who wrote them, what they say, and are they working?

topics.title: The Story of Paper
topics.description: Paper was invented in China in 105 AD and took 1,000 years to reach Europe. This single material enabled bureaucracy, science, and literature.

topics.title: How Electricity Works
topics.description: Electrons moving through conductors power your entire life. From Franklin's kite to Edison vs Tesla — the electrifying history of electricity.

topics.title: The Philosophy of Ethics
topics.description: Is morality absolute or relative? What makes an action right or wrong? Three major ethical frameworks that help us decide.

topics.title: The Mystery of Sleep
topics.description: We spend 1/3 of our lives asleep, yet science still doesn't fully know why. What happens in your brain during sleep?

topics.title: Nuclear Weapons — History and Impact
topics.description: From the Manhattan Project to Hiroshima to today's 12,000 warheads. How these weapons reshaped every geopolitical calculation.

topics.title: The History of Chess
topics.description: Invented in India 1,500 years ago, chess spread to Persia, Arabia, and Europe — each culture changing the game's meaning and rules.

topics.title: What Is a Refugee?
topics.description: 100 million people are currently displaced from their homes. International law, the UNHCR, and what obligations countries actually have.

topics.title: Japan's Economic Miracle
topics.description: Devastated in 1945, Japan became the world's second-largest economy by 1968. How did they do it — and why did it eventually stall?

topics.title: The History of Medicine
topics.description: From bloodletting to surgery with anesthesia. How humans went from superstition to evidence-based medicine over 5,000 years.

topics.title: The Philosophy of Free Will
topics.description: Are your choices truly free, or determined by your brain chemistry and environment? Why this question matters for law and morality.

topics.title: How Viruses Work
topics.description: Not quite alive, not quite dead. Viruses hijack your cells to replicate. How they spread, mutate, and why some become pandemics.

topics.title: The Mughal Empire
topics.description: From Babur to Aurangzeb, the Mughals ruled most of South Asia for 300 years. Their art, architecture, and governance shaped modern India.
